kompyuter.text: English 'computer' respelled to Filipino sound — a teaching word.

tsokolate.text: Spanish 'chocolate' becomes ts- and k — chocolate.

iskedyul.text: English 'schedule' respelled — the spelling that fights English.

titser.text: English 'teacher' spelled as it sounds — a teacher.

kapatid.text: One word for sibling — brother or sister, no gender.

mag-aral.text: mag- (verb) + aral (study); the hyphen marks a glottal stop.

pag-ibig.text: pag- + ibig (love); the hyphen is a glottal catch before the vowel.
